title: Add routing-shadow provenance and review-disagreement observability
goal: Extend the no-effect routing-shadow capture contract so the readiness gate can measure independent semantic-review disagreement, distinguish production cases from test/synthetic/quarantined data, and separate execution aborts from infrastructure failures without rewriting historical cohort truth or introducing any routing or training authority.

acceptance.assertion: Introduce a reviewed versioned capture/record contract extension that preserves existing create-only v2 history unchanged and readable; legacy records with absent new fields remain explicitly unobservable and cannot be silently treated as PASS-quality evidence.

The capture model can bind at least two independently attributable semantic assessments, or an equivalently reviewable privacy-preserving assessment identity contract, so disagreement rate and missing independent review can be computed without promoting lifecycle success into a semantic label.

Every new prospective case carries explicit bounded provenance sufficient to distinguish natural production operator cases from test, synthetic and quarantined data; non-production classes cannot enter the T004 eligible treatment denominator.

Capture records execution provenance separately from semantic outcome so normal completion, execution abort and infrastructure failure remain distinguishable and selection-bias accounting can include unsealed or failed cases without fabricating semantic labels.

New provenance and review fields are bounded and privacy-minimized, exclude raw prompts/transcripts/private notes/unbounded argv, and preserve proposal_only=true with routing, policy, queue, merge, deploy and runtime effects false.
Identity, deduplication, restart/reconcile and conflicting-duplicate behavior remain fail-closed under the extended schema; no migration rewrites or mutates historical cohort receipts in place.

Completion only makes the missing T004 quality signals measurable. It does not authorize model training or unblock T002; T004 must be rerun against the preregistered criteria and return a separately reviewed PASS.

Focused compatibility, provenance, disagreement, privacy, no-effect and failure-accounting tests plus relevant repository validation and CI pass on the exact final reviewed diff before merge and runtime deployment.

required_observability_gaps: execution abort versus infrastructure failure provenance
